levels.title: Absolute Beginner
levels.milestone: Greet someone, introduce yourself, and survive a café order entirely in French.
canDo: Pronounce French sounds (nasal vowels, silent endings, liaison) recognisably.
Use bonjour / merci / s'il vous plaît / au revoir naturally.
Say your name, age, and nationality; count to 20.

levels.title: Beginner
levels.milestone: Hold a simple back-and-forth about yourself, your day, and your needs using present tense.
canDo: Conjugate être, avoir, aller, faire and regular -ER verbs in the present.
Form questions and negatives (est-ce que, ne…pas).
Talk about the immediate future with aller + infinitive.
Handle daily situations: shopping, directions, ordering, small talk.

levels.title: Elementary
levels.milestone: Narrate past events and describe routines and plans in connected sentences.
canDo: Use the passé composé and imparfait to talk about the past.
Describe people, places and habits with adjectives that agree.
Manage most travel and everyday transactions confidently.

levels.title: Intermediate
levels.milestone: Hold a real conversation, give opinions, and handle unexpected situations while travelling.
canDo: Express opinions, hopes and plans; justify them simply.
Use the futur simple, conditional, and common subjunctive triggers.
Follow the main points of clear standard speech and TV.

levels.title: Upper Intermediate
levels.milestone: Discuss abstract topics fluently and follow native media without constant translation.
canDo: Argue a point of view with nuance and appropriate register.
Understand films, news and podcasts aimed at native speakers.
Write clear, detailed texts and emails on a range of subjects.

levels.title: Advanced
levels.milestone: Use French flexibly and effectively for social, academic and professional life.
canDo: Express yourself spontaneously with few obvious searches for words.
Understand demanding, long texts and implicit meaning.
Use idiom and register deliberately. (C2 native-level mastery is out of scope.)
